title: MCP Architecture, Negotiation, and Trust Boundaries · Protocol Recall
summary: Trace one tool call across host, client, server, and external system. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an MCP architecture and threat-boundary diagram after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish host, per-server client, server, model, user, and external service responsibilities.
Trace initialization, capability negotiation, requests, responses, notifications, and errors.
Advertise and negotiate only implemented protocol capabilities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

stimulus.title: Spec Versioning case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case for agent ext mcp architecture that tests this competency: Target a named stable specification and isolate release-candidate changes.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: The newest announced release candidate is already the stable protocol.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Target a named stable specification and isolate release-candidate changes. Artifact under review: an MCP architecture and threat-boundary diagram.

options.text: The Spec Versioning record says an MCP architecture and threat-boundary diagram looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve separate evidence from confidence.

For separate evidence from confidence, apply Spec Versioning without its stated preconditions, omit the an MCP architecture and threat-boundary diagram trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Spec Versioning for an MCP architecture and threat-boundary diagram.
The record names the assumption, applies Spec Versioning, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Target a named stable specification and isolate release-candidate changes.
